macro_segments.title: Two Witnesses from Abroad: When Must They Specify Bifnei Nechtav U'Bifnei Nechtam?

micro_segments.title: Rabba bar bar Chana's Ruling: Two from Medinat Hayam Need Not State Bifnei Nechtav U'Bifnei Nechtam
Logical Proof: They Can Serve as Edei Mesirah (Delivery Witnesses)
The Chaveirim Story and Historical Comparison of Persians vs. Romans
Rabbi Ami in Name of Rabbi Yochanan: The Case Refined to Get Yotzei Mitachat Yedei Edei Ktivah
Rav Assi's Challenge: How Does This Apply to the Case of Two Saying Bifneinu vs. One Saying Bifnei?
Resolving the Tension: Final Position That Two from Abroad Need Not State Bifnei Nechtav U'Bifnei Nechtam

macro_segments.title: Get Mukdam (Predated Get): Why a Date Is Needed and When Predating Is Permissible

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Predated Get (Written One Day, Signed Another Day or Year)
The Machlokes in the Mishnah: Tanna Kamma Forbids, Rabbi Shimon Permits Predated Gets (Except for Gittin Nashim)
Why Is a Date Needed? Rabbi Yochanan's Reason: Shema Yechapeh Al Bas Achoto (Protection Against Covering Up Adultery)
Reish Lakish's Reason: Peirot (Husband's Rights to Produce Must Be Clearly Defined)
Why Reish Lakish Does Not Hold Like Rabbi Yochanan: The Bas Achoto Case Is Uncommon
Why Rabbi Yochanan Does Not Hold Like Reish Lakish: Husband Retains Peirot Until Shat Netina (Delivery)
Understanding Rabbi Shimon Through Reish Lakish: Predating Permitted Because Husband Loses All Rights at Ketiva
Understanding Rabbi Shimon Through Rabbi Yochanan: He Does Not Hold Like Tanna Kamma; Works Only for Tanna Kamma's Rationale
The Dispute Over Peirot Timing: Tanna Kamma Holds Until Chatima; Rabbi Shimon Holds Until Ketiva
Alternate Version: When Do Peirot Come Into Effect? Yochanan Says Ketiva, Reish Lakish Says Netina

Machloket Between Tanna Kamma and Rabbi Yehuda/Rabbi Shimon
Shema Yechapeh Al Bas Achoto (Concern About Protecting a Niece by Predating Divorce from Adultery)
Rabbi Yochanan vs. Reish Lakish (Dispute Over Reason for Date in Get)
Ein Lo Zman Ktivah (Husband Has No Rights from Writing)
Yesh Lo Peirot Ad Zman Netina (Husband Has Produce Rights Until Delivery)
Gittin Nashim (Divorce Documents - Exception to Rabbi Shimon's Rule)
